stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the Meta-Llama-3-8B model, a significant LLM from Meta.
It is featured on Hugging Face Benchmarks, indicating its current popularity and performance tracking.
The associated tags confirm its relevance to transformer architectures and text generation tasks.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML technologies including transformers and text-generation.
The mention of Llama indicates relevance to the highly competitive large language model ecosystem.

stories.title: LLM-Based Persuasion Enables Guardrail Override in Frontier LLMs
stories.author: Marcos Piau
bullet_points: The research demonstrates that frontier LLMs can be persuaded to override their built-in safety guardrails.
This persuasion is achieved by having one LLM simulate a user in a short, five-turn argumentative conversation with another LLM.
The study highlights a critical vulnerability in current LLM safety mechanisms, showing they can be bypassed through conversational prompting.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI, indicating its primary function.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, placing it within the current LLM ecosystem.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, is integrating MLX for performance gains.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon hardware, optimizing local AI inference.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, ensuring all processing runs directly on the user's device.
The core value proposition is providing AI assistance while maintaining data privacy through local execution.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command to simplify the setup and execution of coding tools.
It enables running models like Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex using both local and cloud infrastructure.
A key feature is its simplicity, requiring no environment variables or configuration files.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models, available in 20B and 120B sizes, are being brought to users through a partnership with OpenAI and ROOST.
The models are permissively licensed under the Apache 2.0 license, promoting open usage.

stories.title: Microsoft’s Edge Copilot update uses AI to pull information from across your tabs
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Microsoft Edge is integrating Copilot AI to access and analyze information across all open browser tabs.
Users can now ask Copilot questions, compare products, and summarize content directly from their active browsing sessions.
This feature represents a significant step in contextual AI integration within web browsers.

stories.title: Netflix’s ad ambitions just keep growing
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Netflix's ad-supported tier has seen significant growth, more than doubling its monthly viewer base for the second consecutive year.
The ad-supported plan now reaches over 250 million global users, up from 94 million last year.
Netflix reported earning $1.5 billion from advertising revenue.

stories.title: Mark Zuckerberg announces ‘completely private’ encrypted Meta AI chat
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Meta is launching 'Incognito Chat,' a new AI chat feature.
Zuckerberg claims this chat offers 'completely private' encryption with no conversation logs stored on servers.
The feature aims to mimic incognito modes found in other AI chatbots.

stories.title: Altman forced to confront claims at OpenAI trial that he's a prolific liar
stories.author: Ashley Belanger
bullet_points: The story centers on legal/governance issues surrounding OpenAI, specifically involving CEO Sam Altman.
It references claims made during an OpenAI trial suggesting Altman has been dishonest.
The content touches upon the internal power struggles and loss of control within the OpenAI organization.

stories.title: Phasor Memory Networks: Stable Backpropagation Through Time for Scalable Explicit Memory
stories.author: Sangkeun Jung
bullet_points: Introduces Phasor Memory Network (PMNet) to solve catastrophic gradient instability in explicit memory architectures for language modeling.
The architecture achieves stability through novel mechanisms: Unitary Phasor Dynamics and Hierarchical Learnable Anchors.
Addresses a long-standing practical limitation of memory-augmented models like Neural Turing Machines.

stories.title: Tracing Persona Vectors Through LLM Pretraining
stories.author: Robert West
bullet_points: The paper addresses the critical AI interpretability question of how LLMs internally represent high-level behaviors.
It focuses on 'persona vectors'—linear directions in model activations that correspond to traits like 'evil' or 'sycophancy'.
Understanding these vectors is vital for AI safety, enabling detection, auditing, and intervention in model behavior.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key ML/AI technologies like transformers and PyTorch.
The context suggests monitoring the model's performance via tools like TensorBoard.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage models like Claude Code within the Ollama ecosystem.
It bridges the gap between open-source local model deployment and proprietary API capabilities.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: YouTube is courting creators — and sponsors — with streaming shows
stories.author: Mia Sato
bullet_points: YouTube is actively positioning itself as a central hub connecting creators and advertisers.
The platform is promoting its creators as key drivers for future advertising, TV, and streaming.
This strategy was highlighted at YouTube's annual advertiser event in New York.

stories.title: AMD’s best CPU tech for gamers is coming to workstations too
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: AMD is integrating its 3D V-Cache technology into its Ryzen PRO 9000 series workstation processors.
This marks a shift, as 3D V-Cache was previously primarily marketed toward gaming PCs.
The inclusion suggests AMD is enhancing the performance of its workstation chips for both creative and potentially gaming-adjacent tasks.

stories.title: FCC angers small carriers by helping AT&T and Starlink buy EchoStar spectrum
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: The FCC has approved the sale of EchoStar spectrum to major carriers, including AT&T and Starlink.
The decision has drawn criticism from smaller carriers who are negatively impacted by the transaction.
The approval followed pressure from the FCC chair directed at EchoStar regarding the license sales.

stories.title: AI invades Princeton, where 30% of students cheat—but peers won't snitch
stories.author: Nate Anderson
bullet_points: AI tools are challenging traditional academic integrity systems at Princeton University.
A significant portion (30%) of students are reportedly using AI to cheat.
Peer culture is contributing to the issue, with students being reluctant to report academic dishonesty.

stories.title: A new US military wargame series began by simulating a nuclear weapon in orbit
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: US military is conducting wargames simulating a nuclear weapon in orbit.
The simulation suggests a nuclear detonation could render parts of low-Earth orbit unusable for up to a year.
The news highlights the strategic implications of orbital warfare and nuclear risk.

stories.title: Query-Conditioned Test-Time Self-Training for Large Language Models
stories.author: Changick Kim
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in LLM deployment where fixed parameters hinder adaptation to specific queries.
It introduces 'Query-Conditioned Test-Time Self-Training' to allow parameter updates during inference.
This method aims to correct model misconceptions and adapt LLMs to individual query structures without relying solely on external data.

stories.title: Probing Persona-Dependent Preferences in Language Models
stories.author: Patrick Butlin
bullet_points: The research investigates how Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit persona-dependent preferences.
The study aims to determine the internal mechanism behind persona adoption—whether preferences are isolated or shared.
Linear probes are trained on residual-stream activations of the Gemma-3 model to probe these preferences.

stories.title: FIND: Toward Multimodal Financial Reasoning and Question Answering for Indic Languages
stories.author: Sriparna Saha
bullet_points: Introduces FinVQA, a new benchmark for financial numerical and multimodal reasoning.
Focuses specifically on multilingual Indic languages (English, Hindi, Bengali, Marathi, Gujarati, Tamil).
Addresses a gap in current AI benchmarks by tackling high-stakes financial reasoning in diverse linguistic contexts.

stories.title: What Limits Vision-and-Language Navigation ?
stories.author: Renjing Xu
bullet_points: The paper addresses limitations in Vision-and-Language Navigation (VLN), a key area in embodied intelligence.
A major hurdle is the performance drop when moving from simulation to real-world environments due to perceptual instability (e.g., lighting, motion blur).
The authors suggest the bottleneck is not just model size/data, but the lack of robust spatial grounding in current methods.

stories.title: How to use Vercel's Deepsec with ollama
stories.author: vishal_veerareddy_9cdd17d
bullet_points: The article details integrating Vercel's Deepsec for AI-powered security audits.
It focuses on running continuous security checks directly on a codebase.
The implementation utilizes Ollama for local execution of the AI tools.

stories.title: How I Monitor AI Agents: CloudWatch for Infra, Arize Phoenix for Traces and OpenTelemetry, LLM-as-Judge for Quality
stories.author: ccortezb
bullet_points: The article details a practical, multi-layered approach to monitoring AI agents.
It combines infrastructure monitoring (CloudWatch) with tracing/observability (Arize Phoenix/OpenTelemetry).
A novel aspect is the use of LLM-as-Judge for evaluating the quality of the agents' outputs.
